mechanism_narrative: A sustained expansion in industrial production signals rising capacity utilization and future corporate earnings, which investors gradually price in over the subsequent month, driving S&P 500 forward returns upward.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC of INDPRO vs 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03, or the signal produces negative average return in triggered periods over the same window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and reduces consumer spending expectations, causing investors to downgrade earnings growth and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points over the prior three months
failure_condition: When trigger is met, 5-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases over any 3-year window, or the signal's average return is not significantly negative at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: When both the federal funds rate and inflation are elevated, the joint effect amplifies discount rate headwinds and reduces real dividend expectations, leading to structurally lower S&P 500 returns over a full quarter.
regime_hint: high inflation environment (CPIAUCSL > 3% y/y combined with FEDFUNDS > 4%)
failure_condition: Rank IC of the combined signal over rolling 3-year windows remains below 0.02, or the decile spread of forward returns between high and low interaction regimes is less than 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts during the early expansion phase of the business cycle indicate strong residential investment and consumer confidence, which reliably predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next monthly horizon.
regime_hint: early expansion (UNRATE below 5% and GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth positive)
failure_condition: In periods matching the regime condition, the signal's Rank IC over 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or the average forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate declines below a neutral threshold, the monetary policy stance becomes accommodative, lowering the discount rate for equity cash flows and lifting S&P 500 prices over the following month.
failure_condition: When trigger is active, 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 40% of cases over any 3-year window, or the average excess return over non-triggered periods is not statistically different from zero at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: The confluence of strong payroll growth and very low unemployment indicates a tight labor market that boosts aggregate demand and corporate earnings with multiplicative effects, driving above-average S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
regime_hint: tight labor market (PAYEMS growing >150k/month and UNRATE <4.0%)
failure_condition: Combined signal Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows below 0.025, or the long-short decile spread in 60-day returns does not exceed 3% annualized out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production rising strongly after a recession signals that the supply side is recovering and capacity is being rebuilt, which investors anticipate as a leading indicator of accelerating earnings, boosting S&P 500 returns over the next week.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery (INDPRO > 100 and month-over-month change positive for three consecutive months)
failure_condition: When regime condition is met, 5-day forward SPX returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) over any combined recovery period, or the signal's hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Retail and food services sales trending upward over time reflect robust consumer spending, which drives corporate revenue growth and supports rising stock prices over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC of RSAFS vs 20-day forward returns is below 0.03, or the average return difference between high and low RSAFS growth periods is less than 0.5% over the target horizon.
